commit.message:
consensus: Include gossip-active nodes in block bitmap so pyramid nodes get rewards

The bitmap was only populated from direct timevote messages (prepare/precommit),
meaning a masternode not directly connected to the block producer could never
appear in it, creating a permanent reward exclusion cycle despite the node
running correctly in the pyramid topology.

Fix: when building the consensus_participants_bitmap for a block, merge the
direct voters (timevote messages) with the gossip-active masternode set.
Gossip-active = seen by ≥ min_reports independent peers within the last 5 min.
This proves the node was online during the block period.

The anti-gaming property is preserved: a node that just joined mid-block won't
have fresh-enough gossip records yet (gossip runs every 30s, expiry 5 min,
cleanup every 60s), so it stays out of the bitmap for that block.

reward eligibility (get_masternodes_for_rewards) continues to read from the
bitmap in the previous block, so the whole chain is:
  gossip/direct-vote → bitmap in block N → rewards in block N+1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(version): derive version from git tag when running from source (#125)

## Problem

When running from source, the version display combined `package.json`
(e.g. `1.10.2`) with `git describe` (e.g. `v1.11.0-dirty`), producing a
confusing mismatch: `v1.10.2-dev (v1.11.0-dirty)`. This happened because
`package.json` is only bumped during automated releases, so it is almost
always stale in a local checkout.

## Solution

Use the git tag as the version source of truth when running from source.
Git tags are already the canonical version marker (set by the release
workflow), so there is no reason to read `package.json` when git is
available.

- Exactly on a clean tag: shows `1.11.0`
- Ahead of tag or dirty: shows `1.11.0-dev (v1.11.0-3-gabcdef1)`
- No semver tag (bare hash): falls back to `package.json`
- Installed via npm: unchanged (uses `package.json` as before, since
there is no `.git` directory)

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing
functionality to change)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactor / chore (no functional changes)

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the existing code style
- [x] I have tested my changes locally
- [ ] I have tested on mobile (if UI changes)
- [x] Tests pass (`npm test`)
- [ ] I have updated documentation (if needed)
- [x] My commits follow [conventional
commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)

## Files Changed

- `src/version.js` — extract version from git tag instead of
package.json when running from source
- `test/version.test.js` — 5 new tests covering: exact tag, ahead of
tag, dirty, no semver tag, fallback on git failure (9 total)

feat: add Pi-Agent detection via process name and breadcrumb scanning (#21)

_This PR description was generated by AMP._ 🤖

## Summary

Adds detection for three new AI agents and fixes a false-positive
matching issue for short process names.

## Changes

### New agents (`src/agent.rs`)

**Pi-Agent** — process + breadcrumbs:
- Process: `pi` (exact match only — see below)
- Breadcrumbs: `~/.pi/agent/sessions/*.jsonl`
- Email: `Pi <noreply@pi.dev>`
- ℹ️ Pi is a Node.js CLI — `process.name()` returns `"node"`, not
`"pi"`. Detection works via `basename(argv[0])` which is `"pi"` (npm bin
shim). [Verified on a live Pi
session](https://github.com/block/aittributor/pull/21).

**Copilot CLI** — process + breadcrumbs:
- Process: `copilot` (distinct from `copilot-agent` VS Code extension)
- Breadcrumbs: `~/.copilot/session-state/{session-id}/events.jsonl`
- Email: `Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>`
([verified](https://github.com/github/copilot-cli/issues/975))
- ⚠️ Placed after `copilot-agent` — `find_by_name` uses `contains()` so
the more specific entry must match first

**OpenCode** — process only:
- Process: `opencode`
- Email: `opencode <noreply@opencode.ai>`
([verified](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/issues/786))
- No breadcrumbs — sessions stored in SQLite
(`~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db`), TODO for future

### Bug fix: `exact_process_match` flag

Added `exact_process_match: bool` to the `Agent` struct. When `true`,
`find_by_name` requires the basename to equal the process name exactly
instead of using `contains()`.

**Problem**: The 2-character process name `pi` caused false positives —
e.g., bash's `argv[1]` containing `pipefail` would match
`"pipefail".contains("pi")`.

**Fix**: Pi-Agent is the only agent with `exact_process_match: true`.
All other agents retain the default `false` (backward compatible).

Also added `Agent::default()` const fn so entries in `KNOWN_AGENTS` only
need to specify fields that differ from defaults, keeping the list
concise.

### `README.md`
- Updated breadcrumb examples and limitations to include Pi, Copilot
CLI, and OpenCode

## Testing

```bash
just run --debug  # Verify Pi no longer false-positive matches on "pipefail" etc.
just run           # Only detects actually running agents
```

---------

Co-authored-by: Amp <amp@ampcode.com>

commit.message:
feat(memory): always-on memory consolidation + inbox watcher + QueryAgent

SOURCE: GoogleCloudPlatform/generative-ai always-on-memory-agent
        https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/generative-ai/tree/main/gemini/agents/always-on-memory-agent

WHY: That project's ConsolidateAgent + IngestAgent + QueryAgent solve 3 gaps:
  1. Our instinct system extracted patterns per-session but never cross-referenced
     instincts across agents/projects. ConsolidateAgent finds cross-agent connections
     (same pattern in 2+ agents = convergence signal) — like brain sleep consolidation.
  2. Memory only got data from explicit memory_store() calls. Inbox watcher adds
     ambient ingestion: drop any file in ./inbox/ -> structured memory_global.
  3. No LLM-synthesized answer across platform knowledge — only keyword search.
     QueryAgent endpoint adds cross-layer synthesis with citations.

ADAPTATION vs original:
  - No vector DB/embeddings — LLM reads all instincts directly (same philosophy)
  - Provider-agnostic LLMClient (minimax/azure/gpt, not Gemini-specific)
  - Text files only for inbox (.txt .md .json .yaml .log .csv)
  - asyncio background tasks (not separate processes)

platform/hooks/consolidate.py (NEW):
  - consolidate_once(): deterministic convergence detection + LLM cross-reference
  - start_consolidation_timer(): asyncio task every 30min (CONSOLIDATION_INTERVAL)
  - Stores in instinct_insights (type: connection|insight|convergence)

platform/memory/inbox.py (NEW):
  - start_inbox_watcher(): polls ./inbox/ every 10s (INBOX_POLL_INTERVAL)
  - ingest_text(): LLM extracts summary+entities+topics+importance -> memory_global
  - Processed files moved to inbox/processed/

platform/web/routes/api/memory.py:
  - POST /api/memory/ingest: HTTP ingest endpoint
  - GET  /api/memory/query?q=: LLM synthesis across memory+instincts+insights

platform/db/migrations.py: CREATE TABLE instinct_insights
platform/server.py: startup tasks for consolidation timer + inbox watcher

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace em-dash with -- in release.yml PowerShell Write-Warning

Windows PowerShell 5.1 reads GitHub Actions temp .ps1 files without a BOM
as CP1252. The UTF-8 em-dash (E2 80 94) gets misinterpreted: byte 0x94 maps
to U+201D (right double quotation mark) in CP1252, which PowerShell treats
as a closing string delimiter. This terminates the Write-Warning string early
and corrupts the rest of the script, causing a parse error on the next
quoted string (Remove-Item -Path "build").

Fix: replace the em-dash with ASCII double-hyphen -- .

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add WORD_WRAP.md — frontend-owned wrapping with MeasureWidth trait

Specifies visual word wrapping as a frontend concern, not a buffer
mutation. Key design decisions:
- WrapMap built per-render by the frontend using its own width
  measurement (columns for TUI, pixels for future GUI)
- MeasureWidth trait makes the wrap algorithm generic over monospace
  and proportional fonts
- ScreenScroll operates on screen rows, not buffer lines
- Gutter shows ↪ on continuation rows, line numbers on first row
- Span clipping reuses existing byte-range highlighting with offset
- Vim j/k unchanged (buffer lines); gj/gk (screen rows) future

commit.message:
masternode: Direct TCP connection is authoritative for is_active, gossip is secondary

cleanup_stale_reports was unconditionally overwriting is_active with a
gossip-report count even when the node had a live TCP connection. This caused
OnChain-registered masternodes to flip to inactive every 60s if they had fewer
than min_reports (2-3) gossip witnesses — which happens with any node that has
only 1-2 connections (common in small testnets and pyramid leaf nodes).

Fix: snapshot connected peers at the start of each cleanup cycle and skip the
gossip-based flip for any masternode that is directly connected. Gossip still
controls is_active for nodes we have no direct connection to (the normal
Sybil-resistance case). Pass peer_registry into start_report_cleanup and
cleanup_stale_reports for this check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: Add noclone to DSL_NOINLINE, restore blend_over noinline

Add __attribute__((noclone)) alongside noinline in the DSL_NOINLINE
macro.  GCC 14 was creating constprop clones of noise2, noise3, and
blend_over despite noinline, causing register pressure and I-cache
thrash on the ESP32's 32KB I-cache.

Also restores DSL_NOINLINE on dsl_blend_over() (was changed to inline
in 54ce7b9), which prevented the macro from taking effect.

  Before: 89.9 ms display, 10.9 FPS
  After:  34.2 ms display, 27.9 FPS (2.6× faster)
